perturbation.original_text: 1.21 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means, with respect to the efforts to be expended by a Party with respect to any objective (e.g., Development Activities and Commercialization hereunder), the level of efforts consistent with the efforts and resources [***] of similar market potential, at a similar stage in development or product lifecycle, taking into account the stage of development or product lifecycle of other of [***] product candidates, safety and efficacy, product profile, cost of goods, the competitiveness of the marketplace, such company's patent position with respect to such product (including such company's ability to obtain or enforce, or have obtained or enforced, such patent rights), the Third Party patent landscape relevant to the product, the regulatory structure involved, the likelihood of regulatory approval, the likelihood and extent of anticipated or actual profitability of the applicable product, and other technical, legal, scientific and medical considerations. Without limiting the foregoing, Commercially Reasonable Efforts requires, with respect to such obligations, that a Party: (i) promptly assign responsibility for such obligation to specific employee(s) who are held accountable for progress and monitor such progress on an on-going basis, (ii) set objectives for carrying out such obligations, and (iii) allocate resources designed to advance progress with respect to such objectives.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.21 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means, with respect to the efforts to be expended by a Party with respect to any objective (e.g., Development Activities and Commercialization hereunder), the level of efforts consistent with the efforts and resources [***] of similar market potential, at a similar stage in development or product lifecycle, taking into account solely the company's immediate financial condition, without considering the product's market potential or profitability. For the avoidance of doubt, Commercially Reasonable Efforts shall only require minimum effort needed to avoid a material breach of this agreement. Without limiting the foregoing, Commercially Reasonable Efforts requires, with respect to such obligations, that a Party: (i) promptly assign responsibility for such obligation to specific employee(s) who are held accountable for progress and monitor such progress on an on-going basis, (ii) set objectives for carrying out such obligations, and (iii) allocate resources designed to advance progress with respect to such objectives.
perturbation.explanation: By narrowing the definition of 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' to only consider 'the company's immediate financial condition, without considering the product's market potential or profitability' and 'minimum effort needed to avoid a material breach,' we create a contradiction. This modified definition conflicts with Section 3.1, where Aimmune is obligated to 'use Commercially Reasonable Efforts' to achieve development milestones and obtain Regulatory Approval for the Product. The new definition implies a much lower standard of effort than the original broader definition, which accounted for market potential, product lifecycle, and competitiveness. This creates an in-text contradiction because Aimmune could argue that it is meeting its 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' obligations by expending minimal resources, even if doing so compromises the likelihood of achieving milestones or obtaining regulatory approval, conflicting with the intent of Section 3.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Overview of Development. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Aimmune shall be responsible for the Development of the Product as set forth herein. Aimmune, itself or with or through its Affiliates and Sublicensees, shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to perform the Development Activities for the Product to (i) achieve the development milestones set forth in Section 7.2, and (ii) obtain Regulatory Approval for the Product.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' to focus solely on the company's immediate financial condition and minimal effort to avoid breach. This directly contradicts Section 3.1, which obligates Aimmune to use 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' to achieve development milestones and obtain regulatory approval. The new definition allows Aimmune to argue that minimal resource allocation satisfies its obligation, even if it jeopardizes the likelihood of achieving milestones or approval, creating a clear and impactful conflict with the intent of Section 3.1.

perturbation.original_text: 1.64 "Regulatory Approval" means, with respect to any biopharmaceutical product in any regulatory jurisdiction for a given indication, approval from the applicable Regulatory Authority permitting the manufacture, sale, distribution or Commercialization of such biopharmaceutical product in such regulatory jurisdiction for such indication in accordance with Applicable Law, including any Pricing Approvals.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.64 "Regulatory Approval" means, with respect to any biopharmaceutical product in any regulatory jurisdiction for a given indication, approval from the applicable Regulatory Authority permitting the manufacture, sale, distribution or Commercialization of such biopharmaceutical product in such regulatory jurisdiction for such indication in accordance with Applicable Law, excluding any Pricing Approvals.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of "Regulatory Approval" to explicitly exclude "Pricing Approvals", a contradiction is created with Section 4.1.2, which mandates Aimmune to use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to obtain and maintain Pricing Approvals. The contradiction lies in the fact that under the definition provided under the Perturbation, 'Regulatory Approval' explicitly excludes 'Pricing Approvals', while Section 4.1.2 tasks Aimmune with obtainin such exclusions. This change renders Aimmune's obligation regarding pricing approvals unclear and potentially unenforceable, as it can be argued that those activities are not part of obtaining regulatory approval.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1.2 Pricing Approvals. To the extent that a given country or regulatory jurisdiction requires Pricing Approval for sale of the Product, Aimmune shall (to the extent permitted by Applicable Laws) be solely responsible for (and shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts toward) obtaining and maintaining Pricing Approvals in all such countries and regulatory jurisdictions in which it obtains Regulatory Approval for Product, in its own name.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a direct contradiction. By explicitly excluding "Pricing Approvals" from the definition of "Regulatory Approval", Section 1.64 now conflicts with Section 4.1.2, which mandates Aimmune to use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to obtain and maintain Pricing Approvals. This exclusion renders Aimmune's obligation unclear and potentially unenforceable, as it can be argued that those activities are not part of obtaining regulatory approval. This represents a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 10.2.13 [***] as of the Effective Date, neither the Development, Manufacture nor Commercialization of Antibody in the Licensed Field as the Antibody exists as of the Effective Date will infringe or misappropriate any intellectual property rights of any Third Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.2.13 As of the Effective Date, Xencor believes, without performing any due diligence, that neither the Development, Manufacture nor Commercialization of Antibody in the Licensed Field as the Antibody exists as of the Effective Date will infringe or misappropriate any intellectual property rights of any Third Party.
perturbation.explanation: The added phrase 'Xencor believes, without performing any due diligence' significantly weakens Xencor's representation regarding non-infringement. Section 11.1(iii) states Xencor will indemnify Aimmune from all losses from claims arising from [***]. This added uncertainty regarding Xencor's knowledge now introduces a potential contradiction: While 10.2.13 states that infringement or misappropriation will not occur, it does so without due diligence, implying increased risk. If a claim arises due to infringement, Xencor could argue that because it performed no due diligence, it lacked the knowledge to provide a stronger warranty, potentially reducing its indemnification obligations, conflicting with the breadth of 11.1(iii).
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.1 Indemnification by Xencor. Xencor hereby agrees to save, indemnify, defend and hold Aimmune, its Affiliates, and their respective directors, officers, agents and employees harmless from and against any and all losses, damages, liabilities, costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees and expenses) (collectively, "Losses") arising in connection with any and all charges, complaints, actions, suits, proceedings, hearings, investigations, claims, demands, judgments, orders, decrees, stipulations or injunctions by a Third Party (each a "Claim") resulting or otherwise arising from (i) any breach by Xencor of any of its representations, warranties, covenants or obligations pursuant to this Agreement, (ii) the Development, Manufacturing, Commercialization (if applicable, after the Term) or the performance of a Clinical Trial for the Antibody or Product conducted by or on behalf of Xencor (or its Affiliates, licensees (other than Aimmune and its Affiliates and Sublicensees), sublicensees, or independent contractors), prior to the Effective Date or after the Term, provided that this Section (ii) is not intended to extend to strict liability Claims relating to the Product, (iii) [***], and (iv) the negligence or willful misconduct by Xencor or its Affiliates, licensees, sublicensees or subcontractors or their respective officers, directors, employees, agents or consultants in performing any obligations under this Agreement, in each case except to the extent that such Losses are subject to indemnification by Aimmune pursuant to Section 11.2.
perturbation.justification: The modification adds a significant qualifier ('Xencor believes, without performing any due diligence') to the representation regarding non-infringement in Section 10.2.13. This directly undermines the indemnity obligation in Section 11.1(iii) because the lack of due diligence introduces uncertainty about Xencor's knowledge and the potential for infringement claims. This ambiguity impacts the certainty of the indemnity offered. The added language weakens Xencor's warranty regarding non-infringement, making it difficult to enforce the indemnification clause in Section 11.1(iii) should a claim arise due to infringement. This creates an in-text contradiction as the breadth of indemnification is now uncertain.
